pattern: Every allowed chunk-to-chunk conversion is a directed, weighted edge between two exact strings, so the cheapest way to turn one chunk into another is a shortest-path question, and stitching whole chunks left to right across the string is a small dynamic program on top of it.
hints: Because edits on disjoint ranges never conflict, and the same range can be reused freely, the whole conversion is really about tiling source, left to right, with non-overlapping pieces that each get turned into the matching piece of target.

Treat every distinct string appearing in original or changed as a node, add a directed edge original[j] -> changed[j] weighted cost[j] (when the same pair repeats, keep only the cheapest weight), and precompute the shortest path between every pair of those nodes -- that tells you the cheapest way to turn any one exact chunk into any other, no matter how many single-step edges it takes.

Now walk source left to right with dp[i] = cheapest cost to have already matched everything before position i: if the characters already agree, moving on one step is free; otherwise, for every chunk length that appears among the original strings, if the chunk of source and the chunk of target starting at i are both known nodes, extend dp using the precomputed shortest distance between them.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, several single-character conversions chained together
minimum length one, source already equals target so no operation is needed
minimum length one, no available mapping covers the required change
the direct edge is missing but a two-hop chain of single-character conversions is cheaper than giving up
the same original/changed pair listed twice at different costs, only the cheaper one should count
a single whole-string chunk conversion spanning the entire source

stress.description: small alphabet with a random mix of single- and double-character conversion edges, target derived by mutating a random subset of source positions
larger alphabet with sparser edges (more likely to be impossible), and a fixed multi-hop chain guaranteed to exist among the letters

complexity.time: O(k^3 + n * L) where k is the number of distinct chunk strings (at most 200) and L is the number of distinct chunk lengths
